Petition By Beneficiary Deborah Mosser For: 1) Order Removing Trustee Neveo Mosser; 2) Order Surcharging Trustee Neveo Mosser: 3) Recovery Of Property; And 4) Order Compelling Accounting
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Continued To Jan-04-27 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Frank Cialone, Esq. (via Zoom) for Neveo Mosser and Mosser Group Holdings LLC, Respondents;
Michael Baraket, Esq. (via Zoom) and Jessica Babrick, Esq. (via Zoom) for Deborah Mosser and Derek Massouda, Petitioners (both Petitioners appearing in person);

The Court stated that it had read the joint case management statement and inquired if the parties are willing to continue with the current mediation or do they want to set the matters for trial. Ms. Babrick stated that they wanted a trial date. Mr. Cialone stated that they were okay with a trial date and requested that the trial date be set far enough out to allow for depositions.

The Court assigned the Petition For Orders (1) Instructing Trustee To Notify Beneficiaries Regarding Trust Property, (2) Appointing Temporary Co-Trustee, And (3) Restraining Trustee From Using Trust Assets To Pay For Trustee And Legal Fees And Costs Without Court Approval and the Petition By Beneficiary Deborah Mosser For: (1) Order Removing Trustee Neveo Mosser; (2) Order Surcharging Trustee Neveo Mosser; (3) Recovery Of Property; And (4) Order Compelling Accounting to Dept 206, the Department of the Presiding Judge, for trial call on Monday, January 4, 2027, at 9:30 a.m.

Court to sign the Order Setting Trial In The Department Of The Presiding Judge And Notice Of Trial And Related Orders.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Guitron. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jessica Babrick and Michael Baraket for petitioners Deborah Mosser and Derek Massouda.
Frank Cialone for Neveo Ducraig Mosser (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Petition for Removal, Surcharge, Recovery of Property, and to Compel Accounting; 2) Petition for Instructions, Appointment of Temporary Co-Trustee, and Related Relief; 3) Case Management Conference; and 4) Status of Mediation.

Counsel reported that mediation had been unsuccessful. Ms. Babrick requested that the matter proceed toward trial and raised the need for the trustee's accountings to be filed so that objections could be brought. Mr. Cialone stated that prior requests regarding the filing of accountings had been addressed and that discovery remained ongoing.

The Court set a trial setting conference for May 4, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and directed the parties to file an updated joint case management statement identifying remaining discovery and a realistic timeline for trial prior to the hearing. The underlying petitions were continued to the same date.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Nov-10-2025 Continued To Mar-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jessica Babrick and Michael Baraket for petitioners Deborah Mosser and Derek Massouda, who were also present.
Frank A. Cialone for respondents Neveo Ducraig Mosser and Mosser Group Holdings LLC.

The matter was called for hearing on two pending petitions and a case management conference.

The Court confirmed that mediation was set for February 20, 2026, with a private mediator and that the parties had submitted proposed orders following prior hearings.

The Court first addressed the proposed order concerning loans, which both sides had approved as to form. The Court indicated it would sign that order. The Court then noted that competing proposed orders remained regarding notice and disclosure obligations. The Court stated it would review both proposed disclosure orders, select or modify one, and sign it along with the previously approved loan order.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to March 16, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and set a hearing for Status on Mediation to occur on the same date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Issuing A Breslin Order
Off Calendar Aug-25-2025 Continued To Nov-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jessica G. Babrick and Michael E. Bareket appeared in person for and with Deborah Mosser and Derek Massouda, Petitioners.
Frank Cialone appeared in person for and with Neveo Ducraig Mosser and Mosser Group Holdings LLC, Respondents.
Jake Diiorio appeared via zoom (pro per).
David Stapleton appeared via zoom (pro per).

Discussion was held regarding the sale of 1060 Bush St. which sold below market value, that no information was given to petitioners for the sale of the property until it sold, that Mr. Mosser has made loans to himself, and that possibly suspending Mr. Mosser and having co-trustees put in place.

Mr. Cialone stated that they have a loan deal to remedy the outstanding debt, that he needs to be in control because he is guarantor.

Parties are to meet and confer on giving notice for joint statements on their disputes to be filed by September 8, 2025.

The Court ordered that Neveo Mosser would not make any loans to himself or any entitles he controls.

The Court will issue a Breslin Order.

The Petition for Order Appointing Interim Co-Trustees is denied.

Status on selection of mediator is set for November 10, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

All other matters are continued to November 10, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Reported by Jaime Ply, CSR 13323.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Issuing A Breslin Order
Off Calendar Jun-02-2025 Continued To Aug-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jessica G. Babrick and Michael E. Bareket for Deborah Mosser and Derek Massouda, Petitioners.
Frank Cialone appeared in person for Neveo Ducraig Mosser and Mosser Group Holdings LLC, Respondents.

Ms. Babrick discussed and confirmed the appointment of a discovery referee to assist with discovery disputes, that they would want the accounting to be verified, and that Mr. Cialone to file a freestanding Petition Verifying Informal Accounting being Authorized on Information of Belief.

Mr. Cialone stated to the Court that it didn't indicate a deadline to file the accounting from August 2016- 2023, that it should be complete in 45 days.

Counsel authorized to file a Petition Verifying Informal Accounting being Authorized on Information of Belief set to be heard on August 25, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 approved by Judge Moody.

Counsel authorized to file a Petition for Appointment of Interim Co-Trustee set to be heard on August 25, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 approved by Judge Moody.

Petition to be filed on or before June 18, 2025. The opposition papers are due by July 9, 2025. The motion to be heard on August 25, 2025. The reply to the motion is due by August 18, 2025.

All matters are continued to August 25, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Reported by Michele Lott-Meyhofer CSR 8226.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Issuing A Breslin Order
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Continued To Jun-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jessica G. Babrick and Michael E. Bareket for Deborah Mosser and Derek Massouda, Petitioners.
Frank Cialone and Lydia Davenport for Neveo Ducraig Mosser and Mosser Group Holdings LLC, Respondents.

The Court inquired whether there was any new information beyond what had already been submitted. Ms. Babrick indicated no new developments beyond the existing filings. Mr. Cialone requested confirmation of the protective order, which the Court confirmed it would sign that day.

The parties discussed the potential appointment of a discovery referee to assist with discovery disputes. The Court asked whether the parties had conferred on selecting a referee and encouraged them to either stipulate to a referee or establish a process for selecting one. The parties agreed to meet and confer on a selection process and file a stipulation within 30 days.

Petitioners requested accountings dating back to August 2016, when the trust was funded, arguing that they needed to evaluate pre-COVID financial activity, particularly loans and missing assets. The Court ruled that an accounting must be provided from the trust's funding in August 2016 through December 31, 2019, filling the gap before the previously filed accounting (January 1, 2020 - December 31, 2023).

The Court and parties discussed the timing of mediation and the possibility of issuing a Breslin order. The parties agreed that more discovery is needed before mediation.

The Court continued the matter to June 2, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Parties must file a stipulation regarding a discovery referee by April 7, 2025. A status hearing on this issue is set for April 7, 2025.

The Court also authorized a hearing date of June 2, 2025, for a forthcoming petition for instructions from Ms. Babrick's office. Ms. Babrick was directed to utilize the "notes to clerk" in e-filing to highlight that this hearing date was authorized in open court.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Continued To Jun-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition By Beneficiary Deborah Mosser For: 1) Order Removing Trustee Neveo Mosser; 2) Order Surcharging Trustee Neveo Mosser: 3) Recovery Of Property; And 4) Order Compelling Accounting
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Continued To Jun-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Continued To Feb-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jessica Babrick, Esq., in person, and Michael Bareket, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Deborah Mosser & Derek Massouda, Petitioners.
Frank Cialone, Esq., in person, for Neveo Ducraig Mosser, and Mosser Group Holdings, LLC, Respondents.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the instant petition. The court reminds counsel of the previous calling of this matter and of its order to provide Petitioner with an accounting from 2019 to 2023. There is additional discussion regarding case management. The court indicates that it intends to issue a Breslin order. The court's intention regarding issuing the Breslin order is discussed as well as the possibility of the appointment of a Discovery Referee. The court advises counsel of the option to schedule an informal discovery conference and of the procedure to schedule one. The court advises counsel that it currently does not intend to issue a case management order. There is discussion regarding the statute of limitations. There is additional discussion regarding an accounting. The court reaffirms its previous order for Respondent to provide Petitioners with an accounting that covers the dates from Jan-01-2020 through Dec-31-2023. The court orders the accounting to be provided to Petitioner, and/or filed, on or before Jan-03-2025.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Feb-24-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. A Status of issuing a Breslin Order is set for the same date. Counsel is to file a Case Management Statement, on the Judicial Council form, one week prior to the next calling.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition By Beneficiary Deborah Mosser For: 1) Order Removing Trustee Neveo Mosser; 2) Order Surcharging Trustee Neveo Mosser: 3) Recovery Of Property; And 4) Order Compelling Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Continued To Feb-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition By Beneficiary Deborah Mosser For: 1) Order Removing Trustee Neveo Mosser; 2) Order Surcharging Trustee Neveo Mosser: 3) Recovery Of Property; And 4) Order Compelling Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-09-2024 Continued To Nov-20-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frank Cialone appeared via zoom for Neveo Mosser and Mosser Group Holdings LLC (objectors).
Jessica Barrick appeared in person for and with Deborah Mosser (petitioner).
Stacie Nelson appeared via zoom.
Andrew Smith appeared via zoom.

Discussion was held regarding the accounting, that Ms. Barrick has been seeking accounting from 2007 to current date, that Mr. Cialone believes there's a statute of limitations on how far accounting request can go back. The Court orders an accounting for 4 years back, starting at 2019 through 2023. The Court orders Mr. Cialone to file a Memo of Points of Authorities for Statue of Limitations by November 08, 2024. Case Management Conference is set for November 20, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Parties to file a joint case management statement by November 08, 2024. The matter is also is continued to November 20, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.
